notes: This alias map is intentionally conservative.
Alias entries document scenario-family overlap without upgrading a surface-local row into a canonical direct scenario name.
Only exact canonical names should count toward full shared-scenario contract completion.

aliases.reason: Native header-path filter workload rejects roughly 90% of traffic, but it is not yet normalized to the canonical end-to-end scenario.
Callback-on-accepted filter workload follows the same reject-heavy family but remains a native-local benchmark shape.
Native malformed-traffic workload exercises the same 10% malformed family without using the canonical shared scenario name.
This native stress row covers 10,000-entity latest-state pressure, but not the canonical burst traffic contract.
Snapshot publish pressure is present, but this row is still a native-local implementation benchmark.
Snapshot-buffer pressure exists here, but the row is not yet expressed as the shared canonical stress scenario.
Triple-slot delayed-reader pressure exercises the same reader-lag family without the canonical scenario packaging.
The ctypes force-filter rejection row is in the same filtering family, but it is not yet a canonical shared benchmark scenario.
The ctypes snapshot handoff row shows snapshot-pressure behavior, but it is still surface-local.
Combined ingest-plus-publish pressure overlaps the snapshot-pressure family without being the canonical shared scenario.

Unity runtime verification proves one-entity live loopback Entity State receive, world processing, and spawned-object updates, but it does not yet run the full canonical 10 Hz shared traffic lane under the canonical scenario name.

Unreal proof verification currently proves packaged loadability, orientation, and GRILL-shaped runtime plumbing, but it does not yet publish the canonical one-entity 10 Hz live-traffic row under the shared scenario name on this host.
